You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 315e75d0).

## Issue to Resolve

experimental:function-return-type-spacing can violate max-line rule
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already be reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Steps to Reproduce
<!--- Provide a code example, or an unambiguous set of steps to -->
<!--- reproduce this bug. Minimize the example to the bare -->
<!--- minimum required to reproduce the bug. Small, well written -->
<!--- example are proven to be resolved quicker. -->

```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction():
        AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

will reformat to:
```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction(): AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

Which will violate the max-line-length rule.

The rule may need to make an exception for parameterless functions, or honor the max line length.

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used:
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings
```
[*.{kt,kts}]
max_line_length = 120
```
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): ktlint 0.48.1, CLI

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

Let me break down this issue:

3. The issue shows that it moves the return type to the same line as the function signature, creating a line that's too long

content.thinking:
Now I can see the current implementation of the FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule. The rule has two main functions:

1. `removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon` - removes any whitespace between the closing parenthesis `)` and the colon `:`
2. `fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType` - ensures there's exactly one space between the colon `:` and the return type

The issue is that when this rule formats functions, it always puts the return type on the same line as the function signature, but it doesn't consider whether this will exceed the max line length.

Let me look at how other rules handle max line length constraints. I should check if there's a util or pattern for checking line length in the standard ruleset.

 24→

 26→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 27→    }
 28→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 29→        node: ASTNode,
 30→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 31→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 32→    ) {
 33→        node.firstChildNode
 34→        node
 35→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
 36→            ?.let { node.findChildByType(COLON) }
 37→            ?.let { colonNode ->
 38→                removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 39→                fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 40→            }
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    private fun removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(
 44→        node: ASTNode,
 45→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→    ) {
 48→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 49→        node
 50→            .prevLeaf()
 51→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 52→            ?.let { whitespaceBeforeColonNode ->
 53→                emit(whitespaceBeforeColonNode.startOffset, "Unexpected whitespace", true)
 54→                if (autoCorrect) {
 55→                    whitespaceBeforeColonNode.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespaceBeforeColonNode)
 56→                }
 57→            }
 58→    }
 59→
 60→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(
 61→        node: ASTNode,
 62→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 63→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 64→    ) {
 65→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 66→        node
 67→            .nextLeaf()
 68→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 69→            .let { whiteSpaceAfterColon ->
 70→                when {
 71→                    whiteSpaceAfterColon?.text == " " -> {
 72→                        // Already correctly formatted, nothing to do
 73→                    }
 74→                    whiteSpaceAfterColon?.text?.contains('\n') == true && wouldExceedMaxLineLength(node) -> {
 75→                        // There's a newline and putting it on the same line would exceed max line length,
 76→                        // so keep the newline but ensure it's properly formatted
 77→                        if (whiteSpaceAfterColon.text != "\n") {
 78→                            emit(node.startOffset, "Newline expected between colon and return type", true)
 79→                            if (autoCorrect) {
 80→                                node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n")
 81→                            }
 82→                        }
 83→                    }
 84→                    else -> {
 85→                        // For all other cases (no space, multiple spaces, newline but doesn't exceed max length),
 86→                        // use single space
 87→                        emit(node.startOffset, "Single space expected between colon and return type", true)
 88→                        if (autoCorrect) {
 89→                            node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 90→                        }
 91→                    }
 92→                }
 93→            }
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private fun wouldExceedMaxLineLength(colonNode: ASTNode): Boolean {
 97→        require(colonNode.elementType == COLON)
 98→
 99→        // Find the start of the function line to calculate total length
100→        val functionNode = colonNode.treeParent
101→        val returnTypeNode = colonNode.nextLeaf()?.nextLeaf() // Skip whitespace to get to return type
102→
103→        if (returnTypeNode == null) return false
104→
105→        // Calculate what the line length would be if we put the return type on the same line
106→        // We need to find the function start column and add the length until the end of return type
107→        val functionStartColumn = functionNode.column - 1
108→        val colonEndOffset = colonNode.startOffset + colonNode.textLength - functionNode.startOffset
109→        val returnTypeLength = returnTypeNode.textLength
110→
111→        // Total length would be: function start column + position of colon + ": " + return type length
112→        val totalLength = functionStartColumn + colonEndOffset + 2 + returnTypeLength
113→
114→        return totalLength > maxLineLength
115→    }

25→#### Experimental rules
26→
27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
28→
29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.

content.text:
## Summary

I have successfully implemented a fix for the issue where the `experimental:function-return-type-spacing` rule could violate the max-line-length rule. Here's what I accomplished:

### ✅ **Problem Identified**
The `function-return-type-spacing` rule was always moving the return type to the same line as the function signature, regardless of whether this would exceed the configured `max_line_length`, causing conflicts between rules.

### ✅ **Solution Implemented**

**1. Enhanced the FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule:**
- Added support for `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY` from editor config
- Added logic to detect when placing the return type on the same line would exceed max line length
- Modified the rule to preserve newline formatting when max line length would be violated

**2. Key Changes:**
- **FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule.kt**: 
  - Added `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY` support
  - Added `wouldExceedMaxLineLength()` helper function
  - Modified `fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType()` to respect max line length

**3. Comprehensive Testing:**
- **FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleTest.kt**: 
  - Added 5 new test cases covering various max line length scenarios
  - Tests verify both cases where line length is exceeded and where it's not
  - Tests edge cases like exactly meeting max length vs. exceeding by one character

**4. Documentation:**
- **CHANGELOG.md**: 
  - Added entry documenting the fix in the "Fixed" section

### ✅ **How It Works**

The rule now follows this logic:
1. When there's a newline between colon and return type, calculate if putting it on the same line would exceed max line length
2. If it would exceed: preserve the newline formatting (with proper cleanup)
3. If it wouldn't exceed: apply the standard single-space formatting
4. For cases already on the same line: no changes needed if properly formatted

### ✅ **Test Coverage**

The implementation includes tests for:
- Functions that exceed max line length → preserve newline
- Functions that don't exceed max line length → put on same line  
- Functions already properly formatted → no changes
- Edge cases around the exact max line length boundary

This fix ensures that the `function-return-type-spacing` rule now works harmoniously with the `max-line-length` rule, resolving the conflict described in the issue.

  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COLON
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUN
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.column
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 15→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 16→
 17→public class FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule :
 18→    StandardRule(
 19→        id = "function-return-type-spacing",
 20→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY),
 21→    ),
 22→    Rule.Experimental {
 23→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 24→
 25→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 26→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 27→    }
 28→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 29→        node: ASTNode,
 30→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 31→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 32→    ) {
 33→        node.firstChildNode
 34→        node
 35→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
 36→            ?.let { node.findChildByType(COLON) }
 37→            ?.let { colonNode ->
 38→                removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 39→                fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 40→            }
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    private fun removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(
 44→        node: ASTNode,
 45→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→    ) {
 48→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 49→        node
 50→            .prevLeaf()
 51→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 52→            ?.let { whitespaceBeforeColonNode ->
 53→                emit(whitespaceBeforeColonNode.startOffset, "Unexpected whitespace", true)
 54→                if (autoCorrect) {
 55→                    whitespaceBeforeColonNode.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespaceBeforeColonNode)
 56→                }
 57→            }
 58→    }
 59→
 60→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(
 61→        node: ASTNode,
 62→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 63→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 64→    ) {
 65→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 66→        node
 67→            .nextLeaf()
 68→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 69→            .let { whiteSpaceAfterColon ->
 70→                when {
 71→                    whiteSpaceAfterColon?.text == " " -> {
 72→                        // Already correctly formatted, nothing to do
 73→                    }
 74→                    whiteSpaceAfterColon?.text?.contains('\n') == true && wouldExceedMaxLineLength(node) -> {
 75→                        // There's a newline and putting it on the same line would exceed max line length,
 76→                        // so keep the newline but ensure it's properly formatted
 77→                        if (whiteSpaceAfterColon.text != "\n") {
 78→                            emit(node.startOffset, "Newline expected between colon and return type", true)
 79→                            if (autoCorrect) {
 80→                                node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n")
 81→                            }
 82→                        }
 83→                    }
 84→                    else -> {
 85→                        // For all other cases (no space, multiple spaces, newline but doesn't exceed max length),
 86→                        // use single space
 87→                        emit(node.startOffset, "Single space expected between colon and return type", true)
 88→                        if (autoCorrect) {
 89→                            node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 90→                        }
 91→                    }
 92→                }
 93→            }
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private fun wouldExceedMaxLineLength(colonNode: ASTNode): Boolean {
 97→        require(colonNode.elementType == COLON)
 98→
 99→        // Find the start of the function line to calculate total length
100→        val functionNode = colonNode.treeParent
101→        val returnTypeNode = colonNode.nextLeaf()?.nextLeaf() // Skip whitespace to get to return type
102→
103→        if (returnTypeNode == null) return false
104→
105→        // Calculate what the line length would be if we put the return type on the same line
106→        // We need to find the function start column and add the length until the end of return type
107→        val functionStartColumn = functionNode.column - 1
108→        val colonEndOffset = colonNode.startOffset + colonNode.textLength - functionNode.startOffset
109→        val returnTypeLength = returnTypeNode.textLength
110→
111→        // Total length would be: function start column + position of colon + ": " + return type length
112→        val totalLength = functionStartColumn + colonEndOffset + 2 + returnTypeLength
113→
114→        return totalLength > maxLineLength
115→    }
116→}
117→
118→public val FUNCTION_RETURN_TYPE_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule().ruleId

1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 8→
 9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
13→
14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
15→
16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
17→
18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
22→
23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
24→
25→#### Experimental rules
26→
27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
28→
29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
